metadata.title: Why You Don't Diffract Walking Through a Doorway
metadata.color_semantics: TEAL = quantum / wavelength visible / electron; CRIMSON = classical limit / wavelength unmeasurably small / human
metadata.note: Exclusions honored: no Davisson-Germer numbers, no correspondence-principle formalism, no relativistic momentum. Wonder register: de Broglie scale from first principles — quantum never turns off.
_variant_todo: rewrite every beat narration_text in the Teardown register (voices/teardown/VOICE.md + CLAUDE-BRAND.md) — voice only, facts unchanged
signature tangent 0-1 per video, ONLY on a clear opportunity (see SKILL)
swap the outro to the Claude outro from AUTHOR.MD :: NikBearBrown
then build audience-namespaced (audio in the new voice, scenes in the new palette)

beats.narration_text: med davy A-I. From Sanskrit — meaning intelligent, or intellectually brilliant. The perfect name for our A-I-powered intelligent learning systems.

props.headline: Medhavy AI
props.body:
मेधावी (Medhavy): From Sanskrit, meaning
"intelligent" or "intellectually brilliant"
— the perfect name for our AI-powered
intelligent learning systems.

beats.narration_text: Electrons diffract through crystals. Buckyballs make interference fringes. You walk through a doorway every day — and nothing happens. Quantum mechanics applies to you too. So why don't you diffract?

card.copy: Why You Don't Diffract Walking Through a Doorway
card.sub: de Broglie wavelength and the classical limit

beats.narration_text: De Broglie's formula: a particle with momentum p has wavelength h over p. The same equation applies to everything — electrons, buckyballs, people. The only question is what number comes out.

production_viz.label: λ = h/p — universal, applies to everything

beats.narration_text: An electron at 54 electron-volts has a wavelength of 0.167 nanometers — the same scale as atom spacings. Diffraction happens. A 70 kg person walking at 1 meter per second has the same formula and a very different answer. Why the different outcome?

card.copy: Electron: λ = 0.167 nm — atom-sized, diffracts. You walking: same formula. Why no fringes?

beats.narration_text: Plug in the numbers. A 70 kilogram person at 1 meter per second has momentum of 70 kilogram-meters per second. h is 6.6 times 10 to the minus 34. Divide: wavelength is about 10 to the minus 35 meters.

Ten to the minus 35 meters. A proton has a radius of about 10 to the minus 15 meters. Your quantum wavelength is 20 orders of magnitude smaller than a proton. No slit, grating, or crystal anywhere in the universe has spacings remotely this small.

production_viz.label: scale bar: electron λ → buckyball λ → proton radius → human λ (10⁻³⁵ m)

beats.narration_text: Diffraction only appears when the wavelength is comparable to the gap. An electron fits through a crystal gap. To diffract a person, you would need a slit 20 orders of magnitude narrower than a proton. There is no such slit.

production_viz.label: diffraction condition: λ ~ slit width; human λ has no matching slit

beats.narration_text: Quantum mechanics is not absent for you. Your wave function exists and satisfies Schrödinger's equation exactly. Your interference fringes exist — with a period of 10 to the minus 35 meters. They are simply compressed beyond any possible detection.

production_viz.label: fringe spacing = λ = 10⁻³⁵ m — exists, undetectable

beats.narration_text: Large molecules do diffract — barely. A C60 buckyball has a wavelength of about 2.5 picometers, smaller than the molecule itself. Zeilinger's group made it diffract in 1999 through a 50-nanometer grating. The fringes were real and measurable.

production_viz.label: C60 buckyball: λ≈2.5pm, 50nm grating, diffraction confirmed 1999

beats.narration_text: The classical world is not a different regime of physics. It is the limit where the wavelength is so small that no grating can reveal the wave structure. Quantum mechanics never turns off — it just becomes undetectable as mass and momentum grow.

production_viz.label: as mass grows: λ → 0, fringes unresolvable → classical behavior emerges

beats.narration_text: Lambda equals h over p for everything. Electron: 0.167 nanometers — fringes visible. Person: 10 to the minus 35 meters — no grating exists. Quantum mechanics never turns off. The fringes are just too fine to see.

card.copy: λ = h/p for everything. Electron: fringes visible. Person: 10⁻³⁵ m, no grating exists. Quantum never turns off.

beats.narration_text: med davy. A-I-powered intelligent learning systems. On YouTube, at med davy A-I. med davy dot com.
